mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production signals stronger economic activity, increasing demand for capital and inflation expectations, which pushes long-term Treasury yields higher.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO vs 20-day yield change is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window or the signal does not outperform a random baseline in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% indicates a tightening labor market, which raises wage inflation expectations and prompts markets to price earlier Fed tightening, driving yields up.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day yield change after trigger events is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) over a 2-year period, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer prices and nonfarm payrolls signal an overheating economy, compelling the Federal Reserve to tighten policy sooner, which elevates intermediate- to long-term yields.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (rising CPIAUCSL and rising PAYEMS) does not produce a positive average 20-day yield change at least 60% of the time over a 3-year walk-forward, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPI index below 240), a rise in the CPI index signals that deflation risks have diminished and that the Fed may begin normalizing policy, leading to higher long-term yields.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day yield change after CPI rises within the low-inflation regime is not positive and significantly different from zero (p>0.10) over a 4-year test, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate robust consumer and investment demand, which boosts growth expectations and reduces safe-haven demand for Treasuries, pushing yields up.
failure_condition: If the joint upward movement in HOUST and RSAFS does not predict a positive yield change in >55% of instances over a 2-year walk-forward, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the start of a tightening cycle, leading markets to anticipate further rate increases, which lifts the entire yield curve including the 10-year.
failure_condition: If the 5-day yield change following a Fed funds rate increase above 0.25% is not positive on average (p>0.10) over the first 5 years of the tightening cycle, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP indicates a growing economy that increases capital demand and inflationary pressures, resulting in higher long-term Treasury yields.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 20-day yield changes is below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (below 0.25%), a strong payroll print signals a healing labor market, reducing recession fears and normalizing yield expectations, causing the 10-year yield to rise.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day yield change following strong payroll prints (above trend) during ZIRP is not positive and significant (p>0.10) over a 3-year window, reject hypothesis.
